data.body:
PLAN REVIEW (opus, fitness-for-purpose) — core mechanism is right; risk is concentrated in the write-safety half. VERDICT: not sound to implement exactly as-AC-written; 3 MANDATORY changes first:

C1 (CRITICAL) — write-retry contradiction. _run_acli/_run are GENERIC (cmd: list[str], no read-vs-write knowledge), but AC said 'treat TimeoutExpired as retryable within _MAX_ATTEMPTS' UNCONDITIONALLY. With Jira non-idempotent, that IS the duplicate-write bug. FIX: writes are NON-retried on timeout by default; the read/write decision is made by the CALLER via an explicit flag (e.g. retry_on_timeout/idempotent) — reads (get_issue_links/search/get) pass True; set_relationship/create_issue/delete_issue_link/add_comment pass False. Avoid a brittle in-_run_acli command allowlist. Concrete write-safe deliverable = the already-deferred pre-create get_issue_links existence check at apply_outbound.py:287 + keep _diff_links snapshot dedup + reuse the EXISTING JQL rebar-id:<local> create-dedup in batch_dispatch.create_one (do not reinvent).

C2 (CRITICAL) — retry compounding. Three retry loops exist: _run_acli _MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 (:204), apply_outbound._call_with_retry (batch_dispatch.py:41, retries TimeoutError 3x), batch_dispatch.create_one. subprocess.TimeoutExpired is NOT a builtin TimeoutError. Make the deadline a SINGLE shared budget (not per-loop), and on exhaustion raise a DISTINCT terminal error that _call_with_retry does NOT re-retry — else up to 9x attempts blow the per-pass bound.

I1/N3 (IMPORTANT) — exact kill/reap sequence + the right test. Popen(start_new_session=True, stdin=DEVNULL, PIPE) + communicate(timeout=) + killpg(getpgid(pid),SIGTERM)->grace->SIGKILL is correct, BUT: wrap killpg/getpgid in try/except (ProcessLookupError,PermissionError) for the ESRCH race; AFTER killing call communicate()/wait() again to reap the zombie + drain pipes; capture partial stdout; ensure the timeout path raises BEFORE _check_mutation_failure (never fabricate a success CompletedProcess). TEST with a fake binary that FORKS A GRANDCHILD HOLDING THE PIPE and assert no orphaned process group remains (os.killpg(pgid,0)->ProcessLookupError) — a bare sleep test passes even against a broken bare run(timeout=) and gives false confidence.

Other (lower): stdin=DEVNULL is safe today (acli auth is via its config file, no caller pipes stdin — add a one-line audit note). M1: AC over-claims 'per-pass bounded' — only true given a call-count cap; soften or add a pass deadline. M2: default 120s + env override REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT (acli does OAuth+network, needs more headroom than the 10-30s REST). M3: _advisory_lock/_attestation are GIT subprocesses — only the network git calls (fetch/push, _advisory_lock.py:531-572) need a plain timeout=; don't apply process-group treatment; keep OUT of the _run_acli chokepoint. M4: killpg is UNIX-only — guard with os.name=='posix' or note POSIX-only. N2: route the acli.py:651 delete bypass through the same timeout/reaping. _run_acli IS the right seam.
